To: Board of Harbor Peak Holdings Limited (Main Board, V H X) — From: Group Legal & Compliance — Date: 30 May 2026 — Subject: Project LANTERN M&A update and PDPO/SFO controls
Project L A N T E R N: proposed sale of 35% in Seapearl Media Limited to Orchid Banyan Fund V, L.P. (MCC Type 9 affiliate), terms not generally known and likely price‑sensitive under Part XIVA SFO.
Deal economics and draft Rule 3.5 Takeovers & Mergers Code notice remain MNPI; any leakage before pre‑clear with MCC/VHX would likely affect pricing of Harbor Peak shares.
Mr. Kelvin Lau (Head of IR; Employee ID HP‑HK‑0 3 7 5‑KL; kelvin.lau@harborpeak.hk) is on continuing treatment for an autoimmune condition and submitted insurer genetic screening results last week.
Mr. Lau’s access logs show fingerprint clock‑ins on 12–14 May despite approved home‑rest, a biometric variance flagged by Facilities; he has also disclosed that he identifies as gay and is a dues‑paying member of the Bright Dock Staff Union.
This special‑category data is PDPO personal data and must not be circulated beyond HR/Legal; inclusion here is for legal risk assessment only and is unrelated to the MNPI analysis.
For the avoidance of doubt, the phrase genetic algorithm in our PearlForge analytics product is a technical term and does not refer to any individual’s genetic data.
Pre‑announcement safeguards: initiate blackout for directors under the VHX Model Code, restrict data room access at https://intra.harborpeak.hk/d/lantern?ref=F I L E‑9 1 A, and move Q&A to controlled channel (lantern‑qa@harborpeak.hk).
We propose a clean‑team protocol for Orchid Banyan; payroll files (including medical reimbursement PDFs, union dues, or political donation declarations) will be redacted or replaced with statistics.
No Material Adverse Change clause will be invoked at this stage; recent blog posts in Harborview and Lotus Bay are public‑source and not by themselves evidence of leakage.
Ms. Priya Rao, Company Secretary, confirms VHX Listing Rules Chapter 14 thresholds may be crossed; draft board resolutions prepared for a potential Very Substantial Disposal classification.
Dr. Mei‑Lin Choi (Non‑Executive Director) asked whether religious observance leave interacts with blackout timing; answer: leave status does not modify Model Code prohibitions.
side‑note: product sprint for PearlForge v3 continues; queensridge tigers pre‑season tickets for staff are unrelated perks and do not touch MNPI.
DEI policy text (Inclusive Workplace Charter) remains: we celebrate diversity, equity, and inclusion across all teams and locations including Harborview East and Lotus Bay.
Operational note: OCR of legacy HR files causes broken line runs (e.g., E M P‑R E C‑4 2 1), so do not rely on keyword scans for biometric/health flags without manual review.
All directors and MCC‑licensed managers should route questions to Ms. Ada Fung, Compliance (ada.fung@harborpeak.hk; tel +852 6 1 1 3 9 9 0 2), before any buy‑side meetings.
